SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.util

Class LinkedHashMapWrapper<K extends EqualsModProperty<K>,V>

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.LinkedHashMapWrapper<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - the type of the keys in the wrapped LinkedHashMap
V - the type of the values in the wrapped LinkedHashMap
public class LinkedHashMapWrapper<K extends EqualsModProperty<K>,V> extends Object
This class is a wrapper for LinkedHashMap where the keys are elements that implement EqualsModProperty.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    LinkedHashMapWrapper(Iterable<K> keys, Iterable<V> values, Property<? super K> property)
    Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pairs.
    LinkedHashMapWrapper(K[] keys, V[] values, Property<? super K> property)
    Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pairs.
    LinkedHashMapWrapper(K key, V value, Property<? super K> property)
    Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pair.
    LinkedHashMapWrapper(Property<? super K> property)
    Constructs a new empty LinkedHashMapWrapper.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    containsKey(K key)
    Returns true if this map contains a mapping for the specified key.
    boolean
    containsValue(V value)
    Returns true if this map contains a mapping to the specified value.
    @Nullable V
    get(K key)
    Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key.
    boolean
    isEmpty()
    Returns true if this map contains no key-value pairs.
    Iterator<Pair<K,V>>
    iterator()
    Returns an iterator over the key-value pairs in this map.

    Insert the given key-value pair into this map.

    putAll(Iterable<K> keys, Iterable<V> vals)
    Inserts the given key-value pairs into this map.
    void
    putAll(K[] keys, V[] vals)
    Inserts the given key-value pairs into this map.
    @Nullable V
    remove(K key)
    Removes the mapping for the specified key from this map if present and returns the previously associated value.
    int
    size()
    Returns the number of key-value pairs in this map.

  • Constructor Details

    • LinkedHashMapWrapper

      public LinkedHashMapWrapper(Property<? super K> property)
      Constructs a new empty LinkedHashMapWrapper.
      Parameters:
      property - the Property<JTerm> that is used internally for equality checks and hash codes

    • LinkedHashMapWrapper

      public LinkedHashMapWrapper(K key, V value, Property<? super K> property)
      Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pair.
      Parameters:
      key - the key to be inserted
      value - the value corresponding to key
      property - the Property<JTerm> that is used internally for equality checks and hash codes

    • LinkedHashMapWrapper

      public LinkedHashMapWrapper(K[] keys, V[] values, Property<? super K> property)
      Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pairs.

      The first key in keys is mapped to the first value in values etc. If there are more keys than values, the remaining keys are mapped to null. If there are more values than keys, the remaining values are ignored.

      Parameters:
      keys - the array of keys to be inserted
      values - the array of values corresponding to the keys
      property - the Property<JTerm> that is used internally for equality checks and hash codes

    • LinkedHashMapWrapper

      public LinkedHashMapWrapper(Iterable<K> keys, Iterable<V> values, Property<? super K> property)
      Constructs a new LinkedHashMapWrapper and inserts the given key-value pairs.

      The first key in keys is mapped to the first value in values etc. If there are more keys than values, the remaining keys are mapped to null. If there are more values than keys, the remaining values are ignored.

      Parameters:
      keys - the iterable of keys to be inserted
      values - the iterable of values corresponding to the keys
      property - the Property<JTerm> that is used internally for equality checks and hash codes

  • Method Details

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of key-value pairs in this map.
      Returns:
      the number of key-value pairs in this map

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if this map contains no key-value pairs.
      Returns:
      true if this map contains no key-value pairs

    • containsKey

      public boolean containsKey(K key)
      Returns true if this map contains a mapping for the specified key.
      Parameters:
      key - the key whose presence in this map is to be tested
      Returns:
      true if this map contains a mapping for the specified key

    • get

      public @Nullable V get(K key)
      Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key.
      Parameters:
      key - the key whose associated value is to be returned
      Returns:
      the value to which the specified key is mapped

    • put

      public @Nullable V put(K key, @Nullable V value)
      Insert the given key-value pair into this map.

      If the map previously contained a mapping for the key, the old value is replaced by the given value.

      Parameters:
      key - the key to be inserted
      value - the value corresponding to key
      Returns:
      the previous value associated with key, or null if there was no mapping for key

    • putAll

      public void putAll(K[] keys, V[] vals)
      Inserts the given key-value pairs into this map.

      The first key in keys is mapped to the first value in values etc. If there are more keys than values, the remaining keys are mapped to null. If there are more values than keys, the remaining values are ignored.

      Parameters:
      keys - the array of keys to be inserted
      vals - the array of values corresponding to the keys

    • putAll

      public void putAll(Iterable<K> keys, Iterable<V> vals)
      Inserts the given key-value pairs into this map.

      The first key in keys is mapped to the first value in values etc. If there are more keys than values, the remaining keys are mapped to null. If there are more values than keys, the remaining values are ignored.

      Parameters:
      keys - the iterable of keys to be inserted
      vals - the iterable of values corresponding to the keys

    • remove

      public @Nullable V remove(K key)
      Removes the mapping for the specified key from this map if present and returns the previously associated value.
      Parameters:
      key - the key whose mapping is to be removed from the map
      Returns:
      the previous value associated with key, or null if there was no mapping for key

    • containsValue

      public boolean containsValue(V value)
      Returns true if this map contains a mapping to the specified value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value whose presence in this map is to be tested
      Returns:
      true if this map contains a mapping to the specified value

    • iterator

      public Iterator<Pair<K,V>> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the key-value pairs in this map.

      The pairs in this iterator contain the unwrapped terms.

      Returns:
      an iterator over the key-value pairs in this map
